```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu0    <- sample(c(4, 6, 8), 1)
sigma2 <- sample(c(1, 4, 9), 1)
n      <- sample(c(25, 36, 49, 64), 1)
xbar   <- round(runif(1, mu0 - 1, mu0 + 2), 2)

## Estatistica TRV: -2 log Lambda = n*(xbar - mu0)^2 / sigma2
correct <- round(n * (xbar - mu0)^2 / sigma2, 4)
w1 <- round(n * (xbar - mu0)^2 / sigma2^2, 4)
w2 <- round(sqrt(n) * (xbar - mu0) / sqrt(sigma2), 4)
w3 <- round(n * (xbar - mu0) / sigma2, 4)
w4 <- round(n^2 * (xbar - mu0)^2 / sigma2, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu,`r sigma2`)$ com $\sigma^2 = `r sigma2`$ conhecido, testando $H_0:\mu = `r mu0`$. O TRV resulta em $-2\log\Lambda = n(\bar{X}-\mu_0)^2/\sigma^2 \sim \chi^2_1$ assintoticamente. Com $n = `r n`$ e $\bar{X} = `r xbar`$, o valor da estatistica $-2\log\Lambda$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$-2\log\Lambda = n(\bar{X}-\mu_0)^2/\sigma^2 = `r n` \times (`r xbar` - `r mu0`)^2 / `r sigma2` = `r n` \times `r round((xbar-mu0)^2,4)` / `r sigma2` = `r correct`$.

Note que $\sqrt{-2\log\Lambda} = |\sqrt{n}(\bar{X}-\mu_0)/\sigma|$ e o valor absoluto da estatistica $Z$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S5Q3_TRV_Normal_sigma_conhecido
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
